topic: A coach has to cut a player from the team
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the locker room seemed to hum with an oppressive, irritating frequency. Liam sat on the bench, meticulously folding his worn practice jersey, the fabric bunching under his trembling fingers. Every glance from the assembled team felt like a physical weight pressing down on his chest, making each breath shallow and tight. He kept his eyes fixed on the scuffed toes of his sneakers, refusing to meet anyone’s gaze. When Coach Miller finally approached, his voice was low, measured, and carried an unmistakable finality. "Liam, this isn't working out. Effective immediately, you're no longer with the squad." The words landed in the air like thrown stones. Liam felt a strange numbness spread through his limbs, a cold detachment washing over the sudden, sharp sting behind his eyes. He managed a small, jerky nod, unable to muster any protest, just letting the silence of the room swallow the sound of his own ragged breathing.

[story 2]

I traced the condensation on my water bottle, the chill seeping through my thin hoodie and settling deep in my bones. The air in the hallway felt thick, suffocating, as if the very molecules were resisting my movement. When Coach announced the roster changes, the sound seemed muffled, distant, like listening to a conversation through heavy glass. My stomach twisted into a knot of tight, painful clenching. I could feel the heat rising in my cheeks, a flush of humiliating awareness. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the concrete, but my feet felt rooted to the spot. When the coach looked directly at me, his expression was utterly devoid of warmth, and I could only stare at the scuff marks on the floor tiles, trying desperately to anchor myself against the sudden, dizzying sense of falling apart.

[story 3]

The silence after the announcement was deafening, a vacuum sucking all the noise and color from the room. Sarah stood by the doorway, her arms crossed tightly over her chest, her jaw set hard. I felt a profound emptiness settle in my core, a hollow ache where confidence used to reside. My hands, which had been gripping my bag strap so tightly they ached, slowly relaxed, dropping to my sides as if the effort of holding them had finally depleted me. Every muscle in my body felt heavy, unresponsive, as if gravity had suddenly increased tenfold. When the coach finished speaking, I didn't look up. I just focused on the texture of the worn leather of my shoes, cataloging every imperfection, trying to find some small, tangible thing to cling to in the overwhelming expanse of sudden irrelevance.
